scope_note: QA Runtime ODD Monitor Cert family [264]: second sharp-claim cert derived from the Kochenderfer 2026 'Algorithms for Validation' bridge (Kochenderfer, 2026) §5 ODD-monitor row, anchored at Kochenderfer Validation §12.1 'Operational Design Domain Monitoring'. CLAIM (narrow): for QA finite orbit-class regimes on S_9, ODD membership can be monitored by deterministic orbit-family membership with FP=0 and FN=0 relative to the declared discrete ODD; on continuous observer projections (e.g. (b,e)→((b-1)/8,(e-1)/8)∈[0,1]²) of QA-discrete inputs, a Kochenderfer-style classifier-superlevel-set baseline (Validation §12.1) produces non-zero classification error near orbit-class boundaries that scales with input-noise σ. Claim does NOT generalize to all runtime ODD monitoring; claim does NOT say continuous classifiers are bad globally. The exactness holds only on the QA-discrete side of the Theorem NT firewall. Reuses tools/qa_kg/orbit_failure_enumeration.py orbit_family_s9 (Dale, 2026; cert [263] anchor utility). v1 scope: synthetic-only (declared ODD subset of {singularity, satellite, cosmos}, no HeartMath). v2 may add cert [259] HeartMath as a secondary applied-ODD fixture if the deterministic-vs-classifier comparison stays small. Sources: (Kochenderfer, 2026) Algorithms for Validation §12.1 'Operational Design Domain Monitoring' superlevel-set ODD definition (docs/theory/kochenderfer_validation_excerpts.md anchor val-12-1-odd-monitoring not yet anchored — secondary anchor at val-12-2-aleatoric-vs-epistemic-uncertainty); (Dale, 2026) cert [263] qa_failure_density_enumeration_cert + tools/qa_kg/orbit_failure_enumeration.py utility; bridge spec docs/specs/QA_KOCHENDERFER_BRIDGE.md §5 ODD-monitor row. Authored Will Dale + Claude 2026-04-27. Checks ODD_1+DECL/EXACT/CLF/LEAK/SRC/WIT/F; 2 PASS + 2 FAIL fixtures; self-test ok
